description: Evidence field vocabulary for the Lumina algebra-1 module (gr 9). The complete field set: all six diagnostic law signals plus universal base fields. All law-specific fields are populated by the deterministic algebra parser tool — never by LLM estimation. Law 6 (model_accurately_transcribed) uses an anti-cheat design: the teacher supplies model_params (abstract recipe), and the canonical equation is assembled by SymPy at evaluation time so the student cannot look it up.

response_latency_sec.description: Wall-clock seconds from prompt delivery to response receipt. Universal base field.

off_task_ratio.description: Fraction of the response that does not engage with the current task. 0.0 = fully on-task, 1.0 = fully off-task. Universal base field.

correctness.description: Whether the subject's answer is correct, incorrect, or partially correct. Null when correctness cannot be determined.

hint_used.description: True if the subject's message explicitly requested or acknowledged a hint.

frustration_marker_count.description: Count of frustration signals detected in the subject's message.

repeated_error.description: True if the same error type was made in consecutive turns.

step_count.description: Number of distinct algebraic transformation steps shown in the subject's response.

min_steps.description: Minimum number of steps required for a correct solution to this task (from curriculum).

solution_value.description: The numeric or symbolic value extracted from the subject's proposed solution.

equivalence_preserved.description: True if each transformation step preserves algebraic equivalence. Law 1 (Balance Principle).

reversibility_order_correct.description: True if constant was removed before coefficient was divided. Null for single-step equations. Law 2 (Inverse Operations).

inequality_direction_correct.description: True if inequality symbol was correctly flipped when dividing/multiplying by a negative. Null when no negative divisor present. Law 2 (Inverse Operations — inequality variant).

substitution_valid.description: True if the student's proposed (x, y) satisfies ALL system equations. Law 3 (Interchangeability).

relationship_correctly_mapped.description: True if extracted slope matches correct slope from task context. Null when no slope detectable. Law 5 (Relational Mapping).

structure_preserved.description: True if the student's polynomial expression, when expanded via SymPy, is algebraically identical to the original expression. Catches both distribution errors and incorrect factoring. Null on parse error. Deterministic: set by check_polynomial_structure tool call. Law 4 (Distribution/Factoring).

model_accurately_transcribed.description: True if the student's transcribed equation matches the canonical equation assembled from the teacher's model_params at evaluation time. The canonical form is never stored or shown to the student — only the abstract parameters are provided, making this check cheat-resistant. Null when model_params are absent or unparseable. Deterministic: set by check_model_transcription tool call. Law 6 (Abstraction/Translation).
